---
title: "Robinson-Foulds Distance Calculation: SimPhy Gene Trees vs True Species Tree"
format: html
jupyter: julia-1.10
---

## Robinson-Foulds Distance Calculation: SimPhy Gene Trees vs True Species Tree

```{julia}
using PhyloNetworks
using DataFrames
using CSV 
using Statistics 
include("../../scripts/utilities.jl")
```

## Calcilate RF distance between true gene trees and true species tree 

**Goal:** Calculate RF distances between single-copy gene trees generated by SimPhy and the true species tree.

**Key Components:**
1. **True Species Tree:** `"(A,((((B,C),(D,E)),F),(G,H)));"`
2. **SimPhy Gene Trees:** Individual gene trees with tip labels like `A_0`, `B_0`, etc.

    For n_inds = 1 (one accession per taxa), all gene trees would be `A_0`, `B_0`, etc..., so calculating RF distance btw the species tree is straight-forward. 

    However, if n_inds>1, there are multiple acccession per taxa, all gene trees have tips such as `A_0`, `A_1`, etc, which requires modification to calculate the RF distance (see below) 

This demo will discuss RF distance calculation for both n_inds = 1 and n_inds > 1 separately. 

3. **RF Distance Calculation:** Using `PhyloNetworks.mudistance_semidirected()`

**Algorithm Overview:**
**If n_inds == 1**: 
    1. Read each SimPhy gene tree
    2. Map individual labels (e.g., `A_0`) to species labels (e.g., `A`)
    3. Prune non-overlapping tips between species tree and gene tree
    4. Calculate RF distance and normalize by tree size
    5. Store results for downstream analysis
**If n_inds = 2**: 



This demo shows exactly how the `simulation_postprocess.jl` script calculates these distances.

#### Example data for n_inds = 1: 

The data is created from the below codes: 

```
julia -p 10 scripts/simulation.jl --dup_rate 5e-5 --loss_rate 5e-5 --ratevar G --n_inds 1 --n_reps 10 --n_genes 5  --SF 1 --n_inds 1 
```

The below table shows the normalized RF calculated between true gene trees and true species tree from our pipelibe, simulation_postproess.jl. 

$$
\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\text{gene tree name} & \text{normalized RF} & \text{gene loss only} & \text{Gene loss \& Dup} & \text{nothing} \\
\hline
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene1\_Int1.trees} & 0.667 & N & Y & N \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene2\_Int1.trees} & 0.4 & N & N & Y \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene4\_Int2.trees} & 0.25 & N & Y & N \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene5\_Int2.trees} & 0.0 & N & Y & N \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene6\_Int2.trees} & 0.0 & Y & N & N \\
\hline
\end{array}
$$

### Example data for n_inds = 2: 

The data is created from the below codes: 

```
julia -p 10 scripts/simulation.jl --dup_rate 5e-5 --loss_rate 5e-5 --ratevar G --n_inds 2 --n_reps 5 --n_genes 5  --SF 1  
```

The normalized RF here shows the normalized RF calculated from our pipeline between the true species tree and the true gene trees, which is what this demo tries to test here. 

Then I chose rep1 for this demo, and below is a breakdown of the statistics for those example gene tree files: 
$$
\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
\text{gene tree name} & \text{normalized RF} & \text{gene loss only} & \text{gene loss \& dup} & \text{nothing} \\
\hline
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene2\_Int0.trees} & 0.5 & N & Y & N \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene2\_Int1.trees} & 0.5 & N & Y & N \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene3\_Int0.trees} & 0.4 & N & N & Y \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene7\_Int1.trees} & 0.4 & N & N & Y \\
\text{g\_trees\_noLocusID\_Gene8\_Int1.trees} & 0.0 & N & N & Y \\
\hline
\end{array}
$$

### n_inds == 1: 

```{julia}
# Set up paths using the example data
example_data_path_1 = "/u/b/i/bingl/private/simulation-reptiles/example/RF_calculation/example_output/DUP5.0e-5-LOS5.0e-5-RVG-N_ind1-SF1.0"
example_data_path_2 = "/u/b/i/bingl/private/simulation-reptiles/example/RF_calculation/example_output/DUP5.0e-5-LOS5.0e-5-RVG-N_ind2-SF1.0"
gene_tree_path_1 = joinpath(example_data_path_1, "genetrees_singlecopy")
gene_tree_path_2 = joinpath(example_data_path_2, "genetrees_singlecopy")
```

```{julia}
# Define the true species tree (hardcoded in the simulation pipeline)
true_species_tree_string = "(A,((((B,C),(D,E)),F),(G,H)));"
true_species = readnewick(true_species_tree_string)

println("True species tree:")
println(true_species_tree_string)
println("\nNumber of species: ", length(tipLabels(true_species)))
println("Species labels: ", tipLabels(true_species))

# create some helper functions for this demo 
# Create mapping dictionaries for demonstration using the same logic as the pipeline
# For n_inds=1: A_0→A, B_0→B, etc.
# For n_inds=2: A_0→A, A_1→A, B_0→B, B_1→B, etc.
function create_demo_mapping_dict(n_inds::Int)
    """Create a demo mapping dictionary directly (normally read from astral_mapping.txt file)"""
    mapping_dict = Dict{String, String}()
    for species in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H"]
        for ind in 0:(n_inds-1)
            mapping_dict["$(species)_$(ind)"] = species
        end
    end
    return mapping_dict
end

println("\nDemo mapping for n_inds=1:")
mapping_n1 = create_demo_mapping_dict(1)
for (ind, sp) in sort(collect(mapping_n1))
    println("  $ind → $sp")
end

println("\nDemo mapping for n_inds=2:")
mapping_n2 = create_demo_mapping_dict(2)
for (ind, sp) in sort(collect(mapping_n2))
    println("  $ind → $sp")
end

println("Note: In the actual pipeline, this mapping would be read from astral_mapping.txt")
println("See utility.jl for create_mapping_file_based_on_gene_tree() function.")
println("using create_mapping_file_based_on_gene_tree() from utilities.jl")
```

```{julia}
## Step-by-Step RF Distance Calculation Demo for n_inds = 1 
## Using utilities.jl helper functions

println("CASE 1: Single individual per species (n_inds = 1)")
println("="^70)

# Read and process each gene tree file using helper functions
# gene tree path 1 shows the gene tree path when n_inds = 1 
gene_tree_files = filter(f -> endswith(f, ".trees"), readdir(gene_tree_path_1))
println("Processing $(length(gene_tree_files)) gene tree files for n_inds=1...\n")

rf_distances_n1 = Float64[]
gene_tree_details_n1 = []

for (i, gene_file) in enumerate(gene_tree_files) 
    # loop through all gene trees in this folder 

    println("GENE TREE $i: $gene_file")
    println("-"^50)
    # Part 1: Read the raw SimPhy gene tree
    gene_tree_file_path = joinpath(gene_tree_path_1, gene_file)
    raw_tree_string = strip(readlines(gene_tree_file_path)[1])
    
    println("1. Raw SimPhy tree string:")
    println(" $raw_tree_string \n")
    
    # Part 2: Parse the tree using PhyloNetworks
    gene_tree = readnewick(raw_tree_string)
    original_tips = tipLabels(gene_tree)
    
    println("2. Original tip labels: $original_tips")
    println("Number of tips: $(length(original_tips))")
    
    # Step 3: Use the helper function map_gene_tree_based_on_mapping_file from utilities.jl
    # Note: In the actual pipeline, mapping_n1 would come from create_mapping_file_based_on_gene_tree()
    mapped_gene_tree = map_gene_tree_based_on_mapping_file(gene_tree, mapping_n1)
    mapped_tips = tipLabels(mapped_gene_tree)
    
    println("\n3. After mapping using map_gene_tree_based_on_mapping_file() from utilities.jl:")
    println("   Mapped tip labels: $mapped_tips \n")
    
    # Step 4: Use the helper function prune_nonoverlapping_tips from utilities.jl
    pruned_species_tree, pruned_mapped_gene_tree = prune_nonoverlapping_tips(true_species, 
                                                                        mapped_gene_tree)
    
    final_species_tips = tipLabels(pruned_species_tree)
    final_gene_tips = tipLabels(pruned_mapped_gene_tree)
    
    println("4. After pruning using prune_nonoverlapping_tips() from utilities.jl:")
    println("   Species tree tips: $final_species_tips")
    println("   Gene tree tips: $final_gene_tips")
    println("   Both trees now have $(length(final_species_tips)) tips")
    
    # Part 5: Calculate RF distance using PhyloNetworks
    rf_distance = mudistance_semidirected(pruned_species_tree, 
                                        pruned_mapped_gene_tree, 
                                        preorder=true)
    
    # Part 6: Normalize RF distance
    n_tips = length(final_species_tips)
    max_rf = 2 * (n_tips - 3)  # Maximum possible RF distance for n tips
    normalized_rf = rf_distance / max_rf
    
    println("5. RF Distance Calculation:")
    println("   Raw RF distance: $rf_distance")
    println("   Number of tips: $n_tips")
    println("   Maximum possible RF: $max_rf")
    println("   Normalized RF distance: $normalized_rf")
    
    # Identify missing species
    all_species = Set(["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H"])
    present_species = Set(final_species_tips)
    missing_species = setdiff(all_species, present_species)
    
    println("\n6. Missing species analysis:")
    println("   Missing species: $(sort(collect(missing_species)))")
    
    # Store results
    push!(rf_distances_n1, normalized_rf)
    push!(gene_tree_details_n1, (
        file = gene_file,
        original_tips = original_tips,
        mapped_tips = mapped_tips,
        final_tips = final_species_tips,
        missing_species = missing_species,
        rf_raw = rf_distance,
        rf_normalized = normalized_rf
    ))
    
    println()
end

println("SUMMARY FOR n_inds=1:")
println("Normalized RF distances: $rf_distances_n1")
println("Mean normalized RF: $(round(mean(rf_distances_n1), digits=4))")
println()
```

```{julia}
## Step-by-Step RF Distance Calculation Demo for n_inds = 2
## Using utilities.jl helper functions

println("CASE 2: Multiple individuals per species (n_inds = 2)")
println("="^70)

# Set up paths for n_inds=2 example data
gene_tree_files_n2 = filter(f -> endswith(f, ".trees"), readdir(gene_tree_path_2))
println("Processing $(length(gene_tree_files_n2)) gene tree files for n_inds=2...\n")

rf_distances_n2 = Float64[]
gene_tree_details_n2 = []

for (i, gene_file) in enumerate(gene_tree_files_n2)
    println("GENE TREE $i: $gene_file")
    println("-"^50)
    
    # Step 1: Read the raw SimPhy gene tree
    gene_tree_file_path = joinpath(gene_tree_path_2, gene_file)
    raw_tree_string = strip(readlines(gene_tree_file_path)[1])
    
    println("1. Raw SimPhy tree string:")
    println("   $raw_tree_string \n")
    
    # Step 2: Parse the tree
    gene_tree = readnewick(raw_tree_string)
    original_tips = tipLabels(gene_tree)
    
    println("2. Original tip labels: $original_tips")
    println("   Number of tips: $(length(original_tips)) \n")
    
    # Step 3: For n_inds > 1, we need to handle multiple individuals per species
    # First, get sister relationships and prune related leaves
    sisterdict = get_sisters_for_all_leafs(gene_tree)
    println("3. Sister relationships for all leaves:")
    for (leaf, sisters) in sisterdict
        sister_names = [s.name for s in sisters if !isnothing(s.name)]
        println("   $leaf has sisters: $sister_names")
    end
    
    # Prune related leaves (removes sisters from same species)
    gene_tree_copy = deepcopy(gene_tree)
    prune_related_leaves!(gene_tree_copy, sisterdict)
    
    pruned_tips = tipLabels(gene_tree_copy)
    println("4. After pruning related leaves:")
    println("   Remaining tips: $pruned_tips \n")
    
    # Step 4: Group tips by species to see duplications
    species_dict = group_tips_by_species(gene_tree_copy)
    println("\n5. Tips grouped by species:")
    for (species, individuals) in species_dict
        println("   Species $species: $individuals \n")
    end
    
    # Step 5: Generate all combinations of individuals
    all_comb = all_combinations(species_dict)
    num_combinations = length(collect(all_comb))
    println("6. Number of possible combinations: $num_combinations")
    
    # Step 6: Process each combination to get subtrees
    subtree_rf_distances = Float64[]
    combination_count = 0
    
    for combo in all_combinations(species_dict)  # Recreate iterator
        combination_count += 1
        tips_to_keep = collect(combo)
        
        if combination_count <= 3  # Show details for first few combinations
            println("   Combination $combination_count: keeping tips $tips_to_keep")
        end
        
        # Extract subtree for this combination
        subtree = subtree_for_combination(gene_tree_copy, tips_to_keep)
        
        # Map individuals to species
        mapped_subtree = map_gene_tree_based_on_mapping_file(subtree, mapping_n2)
        
        # Prune to match species tree
        pruned_species_tree, pruned_mapped_subtree = prune_nonoverlapping_tips(true_species, 
                                                                            mapped_subtree)
        
        # Calculate RF distance
        rf_dist = mudistance_semidirected(pruned_species_tree, 
                                        pruned_mapped_subtree, 
                                        preorder=true)
        n_tips = length(tipLabels(pruned_species_tree))
        normalized_rf = rf_dist / (2 * (n_tips - 3))
        
        push!(subtree_rf_distances, normalized_rf)
        
        if combination_count <= 3
            println("     → Subtree tips: $(tipLabels(subtree))")
            println("     → Mapped tips: $(tipLabels(mapped_subtree))")
            println("     → Final tips: $(tipLabels(pruned_species_tree))")
            println("     → RF distance: $normalized_rf")
        end
    end
    
    # Part 7: Calculate mean RF distance across all subtrees
    mean_rf_for_gene = mean(subtree_rf_distances)

    println("7. Final RF calculation for gene tree:")
    println("   Individual subtree RF distances: $subtree_rf_distances")
    println("   Mean RF distance across all subtrees: $mean_rf_for_gene")
    
    push!(rf_distances_n2, mean_rf_for_gene)
    push!(gene_tree_details_n2, (
        file = gene_file,
        original_tips = original_tips,
        pruned_tips = pruned_tips,
        species_dict = species_dict,
        num_combinations = num_combinations,
        subtree_rf_distances = subtree_rf_distances,
        mean_rf = mean_rf_for_gene
    ))
    
    println()
end

println("SUMMARY FOR n_inds=2:")
println("Mean RF distances per gene tree: $rf_distances_n2")
println("Overall mean RF: $(round(mean(rf_distances_n2), digits=4))")
println()
```

The calculation above matches the calculation of my pipeline.
